title: Exploring Sparse Matrix Multiplication Kernels on the Cerebras CS-3
abstract: The paper explores the ability of the Cerebras CS-3 to perform sparse-dense matrix multiplication (SpMM) and sampled dense-dense matrix multiplication (SDDMM), seeking optimizations that improve the time and memory efficiency of these kernels on the CS-3.
research_focus: Optimizing sparse matrix multiplication kernels on the Cerebras CS-3 accelerator

performance_metrics.spmm_performance: 100× faster than CPU for 90% sparse matrices (for large and denser matrices)
performance_metrics.sddmm_performance: Typically an order of magnitude faster than CPU
performance_metrics.bottleneck: High host-device communication overhead for hyper-sparse matrices

main_findings: CS-3 can achieve significantly higher training throughput for DNNs compared to GPUs
For large and denser matrices, SpMM performance on CS-3 can be 100× faster than CPU for 90% sparse matrices
SDDMM performance is typically an order of magnitude faster than CPU
As matrices become hyper-sparse, the CS-3 suffers from high host-device communication overhead relative to computation
contributions: Proposed SpMM and SDDMM CSL kernels for efficient CS-3 execution
Optimized SpMM to support multiple I/O channel data streaming from the host to the CS-3
Implemented support for larger matrices than a PyTorch implementation
Evaluated implementations on synthetically generated sparse matrices to showcase CS-3 performance comparison with CPU
